Specifically, Paraprasophyllum colensoi is classified within the subfamily Orchidoideae. The species is native to New Zealand. As part of the Orchidoideae subfamily, it is further classified into the tribe Diurideae.

intro: Paraprasophyllum colensoi is a species of orchid native to New Zealand. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties.
